heroSubtitle: Find answers to your questions about traditions, visiting etiquette, and using the NusaBudaya portal.
etiquetteSubtitle: Essential guidelines for tourists and visitors who wish to attend ceremonies or visit temples in Bali.
contactSubtitle: Still have questions? Don't hesitate to reach out to our team.
contactAddress: Jl. Raya Ubud No. 88, Gianyar, Bali, Indonesia

faq.question: What is NusaBudaya?
faq.answer: NusaBudaya is a digital portal that presents comprehensive information about traditions, traditional ceremonies, and the cultural calendar of the Indonesian Archipelago, particularly Bali. We aim to help the general public and tourists understand the spiritual and cultural richness of Indonesia.

faq.question: Can I attend traditional ceremonies as a tourist?
faq.answer: Yes, many traditional ceremonies in Bali are open to the public. However, you must follow the dress code and applicable etiquette. Make sure to read the etiquette guidelines on this page before visiting.

faq.question: How can I find the latest ceremony schedules?
faq.answer: You can visit the Event Calendar page on our portal. There, we provide ceremony and celebration schedules that are regularly updated based on the traditional Balinese calendar system (Wuku and Sasih).

faq.question: Is the content on this portal available in English?
faq.answer: Yes! We provide bilingual support (Indonesian and English). You can switch languages at any time using the ID | EN toggle located at the top of the page.

faq.question: Can I contribute to adding tradition information?
faq.answer: Absolutely! We greatly appreciate contributions from the community. Please contact us through the contact form at the bottom of this page to submit your information or suggestions.

etiquette.title: Modest Clothing
etiquette.description: Wear clothing that covers your shoulders and knees. Use a sash (senteng) which is usually available at the temple entrance.

etiquette.title: Menstruation Restriction
etiquette.description: Women who are menstruating are not allowed to enter the temple area in accordance with local customary beliefs.

etiquette.title: Photography Permission
etiquette.description: Always ask permission before taking photos or videos, especially during ceremony processions. Some areas may prohibit the use of cameras.

etiquette.title: Keep Quiet
etiquette.description: Speak softly and avoid making noise while in the ceremony area or inside the temple.

etiquette.title: Donate Wisely
etiquette.description: If you wish to make a donation (punia), do so sincerely without expecting anything in return. Ask the temple priest about the proper procedure.

etiquette.title: Respect Sacred Buildings
etiquette.description: Do not sit on shrine structures, touch offerings without permission, or enter areas marked as restricted zones.
